title: Rayan Ghantous
content:
People.ContactEmail rghantous@mccarthy.ca
- Université de Montréal
Rayan completed a Bachelor of Laws (LL.B.), honors track, at the University of Montreal and is currently pursuing a Juris Doctor (J.D.) at the same university. During his academic career, he received a mention of excellence by the Faculty of Law and the Berthelot award. Rayan is also the recipient of the Spiegel Sohmer excellence scholarship, awarded to the student with the highest standing in tax and commercial law.
Prior to his law studies, he obtained a Diploma of college studies (DEC) in Natural sciences from André-Grasset College, where he obtained a scholarship for his excellence and his extracurricular involvement.
Rayan served as a clerk for a judge at the Court of Appeal of Quebec. He was also a member of the Pro Bono committee at the University of Montreal for three consecutive years. These experiences gave him a deeper understanding of the law and its applications while helping to promote access to justice. Before joining McCarthy Tétrault, Rayan interned with an immigration consultant. As a result, he developed a keen interest in this area of law.
In addition, Rayan is actively involved in the community. Since 2018, he has served as a scout leader in a group associated with Scouts Canada. Eager for new challenges, Rayan participated in a writing project during his last three years in high school, where he authored three novels that were published with the school’s support. This achievement allowed him to hone his French writing skills and nurture his creativity. Following that, the Commission scolaire de Montréal awarded him the Commissioners' Prize for outstanding quality of French.
Rayan has diverse interests and hobbies, including taekwondo, piano, swimming, and Lebanese folkloric dance. In his spare time, he also enjoys reading, writing, travelling, and gardening. He is fluent in French, English, and Arabic.
